core.database.sqlite_adapter module

SQLITE ADAPTER – SQLite implementation of DatabaseInterface

PURPOSE:

Implements the DatabaseInterface for SQLite. This adapter handles all SQLite-specific connection details, query execution, and data insertion.

WHY THIS EXISTS:

  • Separates SQLite-specific code from the rest of the application

  • Makes it easy to switch to PostgreSQL later

  • Centralizes all SQLite pragmas and settings

  • Implements the adapter pattern for database flexibility

UNITS AND ASSUMPTIONS:

  • All time values are in nanoseconds

  • All energy values are in microjoules

  • SQLite connection uses Row factory for dict-like access

  • Foreign keys must be enabled for data integrity

  • WAL journal mode for better concurrency

AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy

class core.database.sqlite_adapter.SQLiteAdapter(config: Dict[str, Any])[source]

Bases: DatabaseInterface

SQLite implementation of the database interface.

This adapter handles all SQLite-specific operations including: - Connection management with appropriate pragmas - Transaction handling - Data insertion with SQLite-specific methods (lastrowid) - Batch inserts for performance - Schema creation with IF NOT EXISTS

close() → None[source]

Close database connection.

commit() → None[source]

Commit the current transaction.

connect() → None[source]

Establish SQLite connection with proper settings.

create_tables() → None[source]

Create all tables if they don’t exist.

create_tax_summaries(exp_id: int) → None[source]

Create tax summary entries for all agentic runs in an experiment.

execute(query: str, params: tuple | dict | None = None) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Execute a query and return results as dictionaries.

Parameters:

  • query – SQL query string with placeholders

  • params – Parameters for the query

Returns:

List of dictionaries, each representing a row

execute_many(query: str, params_list: List[tuple | dict]) → int[source]

Execute a query multiple times with different parameters.

Parameters:

  • query – SQL query string

  • params_list – List of parameter sets

Returns:

Number of rows affected

get_ml_data(workflow: str | None = None) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Retrieve flattened ML data from the ml_features view.

get_run(run_id: int) → Dict[str, Any] | None[source]

Retrieve a single run by ID.

get_runs_by_experiment(exp_id: int) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Retrieve all runs for an experiment.

get_tax_summaries(exp_id: int | None = None) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Retrieve tax summaries, optionally filtered by experiment.

insert_baseline(baseline_data: Dict[str, Any]) → str[source]

Insert an idle baseline measurement.

insert_cpu_samples(run_id: int, samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert CPU samples from turbostat.

insert_energy_samples(run_id: int, samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert high-frequency RAPL samples.

insert_environment_config(env_data: dict) → int[source]

Insert environment configuration or return existing ID.

Parameters:

env_data – Dictionary with environment fields: - env_hash: Unique environment fingerprint - python_version, python_implementation - os_name, os_version, kernel_version - git_commit, git_branch, git_dirty - numpy_version, torch_version, transformers_version - container_runtime, container_image

Returns:

Primary key of existing or new record

Return type:

env_id

insert_experiment(experiment_data: Dict[str, Any]) → int[source]

Insert a new experiment record with all fields including session tracking.

insert_hardware(hardware_data: Dict[str, Any]) → int[source]

Insert hardware configuration or return existing ID. Now supports hardware fingerprint fields and updates old records.

insert_interrupt_samples(run_id: int, samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert interrupt samples.

insert_llm_interaction(interaction_data: dict) → int[source]

Insert an LLM interaction record

insert_orchestration_events(run_id: int, events: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert orchestration events for a run.

insert_run(run_data: Dict[str, Any]) → int[source]

Insert a single run with all its metrics.

This is a simplified version - the full 80-column insert will be implemented in the repositories split.

rollback() → None[source]

Rollback the current transaction.

transaction() → SQLiteAdapter[source]

Begin a transaction. Legacy method - use context manager instead.
